channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Education Department warns schools to cut DEI or lose funding
item.description: Twelve states have already passed laws to DEI efforts, while another twelve are considering similar measures.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Leader behind migrant flight to Martha's Vineyard tapped to head red state's new immigration board
item.description: Larry Keefe helped organize the flight of dozens of migrants to Martha's Vineyard under the Biden administration and will now help lead Florida's immigration enforcement efforts.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress closing in on shutdown deadline with no clear plan
item.description: Congress is struggling to strike a deal to keep the government funded as a looming deadline to prevent a shutdown next month gets closer. Lawmakers are less than a month away from a mid-March date to pass legislation to prevent a funding lapse — or risk the first shutdown in years.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Leonard Peltier leaves prison after Biden commuted his sentence in the killing of two FBI agents
item.description: Indigenous activist Leonard Peltier has been released from a high-security Florida prison. Peltier was freed Tuesday morning, nearly a month after then-President Joe Biden commuted his life sentence in the 1975 killings of two FBI agents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Wisconsin Supreme Court rules Republican had no right to bring lawsuit challenging mobile voting
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A divided Wisconsin Supreme Court ruled Tuesday that a Republican Party official lacked the standing to bring a lawsuit challenging the use of a mobile voting van in 2022.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How President Trump is reshaping transgender people’s rights
item.description: Coming up at 9 a.m. on Tuesday, MPR News host Angela Davis and her guests break down President Donald Trump’s series of executive orders that significantly reshape transgender rights in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Angela Davis and Nikhil Kumaran, MPR News

item.title: 4 NYC deputy mayors resign after meeting with Adams
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- Four high-level officials in Mayor Eric Adams' administration resigned Monday after expressing concerns about the mayor's relationship with the Trump Administration, according to City Hall and sources familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, NewsNation

item.title: Judge sets Wednesday hearing in motion to dismiss case against New York City Mayor Eric Adams
item.description: A federal judge has scheduled a Wednesday hearing in the prosecution of New York City Mayor Eric Adams, ordering lawyers to address the reasons why the Justice Department asked to dismiss corruption charges and the “scope and effect” of the mayor's consent.
item.dc:creator: Larry Neumeister, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DOGE Wars: Ruling expected as states seek restraining order against Musk, Trump quest to slash bureaucracy
item.description: As Elon Musk seeks to root out government waste, his DOGE effort is set to face a key hurdle with a judge expected to issue a ruling on Monday as states challenge the effort.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Social Security head steps down over DOGE data access: Sources
item.description: King's exit from the administration is one of several departures of high-ranking officials concerned about DOGE staffers' potential unlawful access to private taxpayer information.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pro-life movement to shake up messaging with big investment from these key players
item.description: Several pro-life figures are pouring millions of dollars into a new venture fund to push pro-life resources and messaging under a second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico’s President Sheinbaum threatens to take legal action over the Gulf name change
item.description: Earlier this month, Sheinbaum shared a letter addressed to Google with reporters, arguing that the U.S. had no authority to unilaterally rename the Gulf.
item.dc:creator: Manuela López Restrepo, MPR News

item.title: As sports betting has soared, more people search online for help with gambling addiction
item.description: A study shows more people are looking for help to manage gambling addiction, in the years after a Supreme Court decision allowed online sports betting in 38 states.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle, MPR News

item.title: Top federal agency exposed for spending billions on migrants in a single year
item.description: The Department of Health and Human Services distributed more than $10 billion in a single year to nonprofits for the care of migrants, according to a new watchdog report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Carolina bill would ban smoking inside cars while children are passengers
item.description: A South Carolina group of senators introduced a new bill this week that would ban smoking in a vehicle with a child 12 years or younger present.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans hope to jam through defense, border funding boost ahead of shutdown fight
item.description: Senate Republicans have the ambitious goal of jamming through funding boosts for defense and border security — separately from the appropriations process — ahead of a mid-March deadline to prevent a government shutdown. Senate Budget Committee Chair Lindsey Graham (R-S.C.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, NewsNation

item.title: Mike Pence aims to be 'constructive force' during Trump's second term while willing to challenge him
item.description: Former Vice President Mike Pence has billed himself the leading Republican opposition voice to the "more populist thinking" of President Donald Trump's allies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: For a St. Cloud family, Trump's pause of refugee admissions brings worry for Afghan relatives
item.description: One of President Trump’s first actions after taking office was an executive order indefinitely suspending the U.S. refugee resettlement program. A family living in St. Cloud who fled Afghanistan a year ago worries about relatives left behind.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Senator Brian Jones to propose bill that would reform sanctuary state practices
item.description: Senator Brian Jones is looking to propose a bill that would shake up current legislation that shapes California as a sanctuary state.
item.dc:creator: Kasia Gregorczyk, Fox 5

item.title: Trump nominates Ed Martin, an ally who defended Jan. 6 rioters, to stay as top DC federal prosecutor
item.description: Conservative activist Ed Martin, who echoed Donald Trump's baseless claims of a stolen 2020 election and defended Jan. 6, 2021, U.S. Capitol rioters, was nominated by the president on Monday to be the top federal prosecutor in Washington on a permanent basis.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: GOP lawmakers divided on Musk, seen by some as a liability
item.description: Republicans on Capitol Hill are divided over tech billionaire Elon Musk being the public face of President Trump’s aggressive bid to shrink the government, with some souring on Musk’s prominent role as he increasingly has become a target of Democratic attacks.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats grapple for response on education as GOP charges ahead
item.description: The Democratic Party is having to take a hard look at its education strategy as Republicans move aggressively on the issue with satisfaction in public education at its lowest point in decades.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Washington Post backs out of 'Fire Elon Musk' ad order
item.description: The Washington Post this week backed out of a “Fire Elon Musk” advertising order that was to run as a wrap on some of its Tuesday editions, according to the advocacy group Common Cause.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Five pressing questions as RFK Jr. takes HHS reins
item.description: His entire Make America Healthy Again movement is based largely on undermining the scientific and public health messaging from the federal government, along with overhauling the status quo in the food and drug industries.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, Fox 5

item.title: Justice Department's independence is threatened as Trump's team asserts power over cases and staff
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi had insisted at her Senate confirmation hearing that as attorney general, her Justice Department would not “play politics.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel's Netanyahu signals he's moving ahead with Trump's idea to transfer Palestinians from Gaza
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Sunday signaled that he was moving ahead with U.S. President Donald Trump's proposal to transfer the Palestinian population out of Gaza, calling it “the only viable plan to enable a different future" for the region.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE and NATALIE MELZER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump takes laps on Daytona 500 speedway in The Beast
item.description: “This is your favorite president,” Trump said. “I’m a big fan. I’m a really big fan of you people. How you do this, I don’t know. But I just want you to be safe.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: House GOP budget resolution in jeopardy amid concerns from moderates
item.description: The House GOP’s budget resolution could be in jeopardy of not clearing the full chamber amid concerns from moderates over likely cuts to social safety net programs — particularly Medicaid — with a handful undecided on whether they will support the key measure. Reps. David Valadao (R-Calif.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: House Republicans plot impeachment against judges blocking Trump, DOGE
item.description: Some House Republicans are pledging to bring up impeachment articles against federal judges blocking Trump administration actions.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Staff at CDC and NIH are reeling as Trump administration cuts workforce
item.description: As many as 1,300 probationary employees at CDC and 1,500 at NIH are losing their jobs. Many fear for the future of public health and scientific research.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone, Pien Huang, and Rob Stein, MPR News

item.title: Immigration poll shows growing support for restrictions, but deep divisions remain
item.description: A new NPR/Ipsos poll shows growing support for some restrictions on immigration. Still, many elements of President Trump's sweeping crackdown are unpopular with Democrats and independents.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose, MPR News

item.title: Issa Rae cancels sold-out Kennedy Center after Trump takeover
item.description: "Unfortunately, due to what I believe to be an infringement on the values of an institution that has faithfully celebrated artists of all backgrounds through all mediums, I've decided to cancel my appearance at this venue," the Emmy-nominated "Insecure" actor said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Denver Public Schools sues DHS over policy allowing ICE in schools
item.description: Denver Public Schools (DPS) sued the Department of Homeland Security (DHS) on Wednesday over its new Trump administration policy allowing Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) to conduct raids in schools.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Measure for county to back ban on trans women in sports fails
item.description: An effort to get San Diego County to throw its support behind a bill in Congress that would ban transgender girls and women from competing in women's sports failed when it was brought forward at the Board of Supervisors on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Legislature cancels meetings, closes building as Sierra storm arrives in Nevada
item.description: While Las Vegas drivers navigate rain-soaked roads for the first time in awhile, the Nevada Legislature has shut down for the day to keep drivers out of hazardous conditions in the Reno-Carson City area.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kamala Harris would be Democratic frontrunner for California governor in 2026, poll suggests
item.description: Former Vice President Harris would be the Democratic frontrunner for governor of California if she jumped into the Golden State’s 2026 race to replace Gov. Gavin Newsom (D), new polling shows.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nevada Legislature OKs bill targeting high egg prices, allowing suspension of cage-free requirement
item.description: Nevada lawmakers on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that could bring down the price of eggs, clearing the way for Gov. Joe Lombardo to give it his signature.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
